info.name: Quipux Earthquake Monitor API
info.description: Functional test collection for the Quipux Earthquake Monitor API. It includes smoke tests, paginated queries with filters and sorting, response contract checks, and HTTP 422 validation scenarios. All requests use the {{baseUrl}} collection variable.

item.name: 00 - Smoke
item.description: Basic availability checks for root and health endpoints.

request.description: Verifies that the API root endpoint is available and returns the application name. Expected response: HTTP 200.

exec: pm.test("Root response contract is valid", function () {

request.description: Verifies the basic API health endpoint. Expected response: HTTP 200 with status equal to ok.

exec: pm.test("Health response contract is valid", function () {

item.name: 01 - Earthquakes
item.description: Listing, pagination, sorting and filtering for GET /earthquakes.

request.description: Lists earthquakes using the API default pagination and descending sort settings. Expected response: HTTP 200 with the paginated earthquake contract.

exec: pm.test("Paginated response contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Default pagination values are correct", function () {
pm.test("Earthquake item contract is valid", function () {

request.description: Lists earthquakes using the page and pageSize collection variables. Expected response: HTTP 200 with matching pagination metadata.

exec: pm.test("Paginated response contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Pagination metadata matches collection variables", function () {
pm.test("Earthquake item contract is valid", function () {

item.name: List Earthquakes - Time Range and Ascending Sort

request.description: Lists earthquakes inside the startTime and endTime range ordered by event_time ascending. Expected response: HTTP 200.

exec: pm.test("Paginated response contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Earthquake item contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Items are within the configured time range", function () {
pm.test("Items are ordered ascending by event_time", function () {

request.description: Lists earthquakes filtered by minMagnitude and maxMagnitude. Expected response: HTTP 200 with matching earthquake records when available.

exec: pm.test("Paginated response contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Earthquake item contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Non-null magnitudes are within the configured range", function () {

item.name: 02 - Metrics
item.description: Listing, pagination and filtering for GET /metrics.

request.description: Lists stored hourly metrics using default pagination and descending sort. Expected response: HTTP 200 with the paginated metric contract.

exec: pm.test("Paginated response contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Default pagination values are correct", function () {
pm.test("Metric item contract is valid", function () {

item.name: List Metrics - Time Range and Ascending Sort

request.description: Lists metrics inside the configured time range ordered by window_start ascending. Expected response: HTTP 200.

exec: pm.test("Paginated response contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Metric item contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Items are within the configured time range", function () {
pm.test("Items are ordered ascending by window_start", function () {

item.name: 03 - Reports
item.description: Listing, pagination and filtering for GET /reports.

request.description: Lists stored hourly reports using default pagination and descending sort. Expected response: HTTP 200 with the paginated report contract.

exec: pm.test("Paginated response contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Default pagination values are correct", function () {
pm.test("Report item contract is valid", function () {

request.description: Lists reports using the page and pageSize collection variables. Expected response: HTTP 200 with matching pagination metadata.

exec: pm.test("Paginated response contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Pagination metadata matches collection variables", function () {
pm.test("Report item contract is valid", function () {

item.name: List Reports - Time Range and Ascending Sort

request.description: Lists reports inside the configured time range ordered by report_date ascending. Expected response: HTTP 200.

exec: pm.test("Paginated response contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Report item contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Items are within the configured time range", function () {
pm.test("Items are ordered ascending by report_date", function () {

item.name: 04 - Validation Errors
item.description: Requests that must return HTTP 422 due to invalid parameters.

request.description: Sends page=0 to verify that invalid page values are rejected. Expected response: HTTP 422 with a validation detail for page.

exec: pm.test("Validation error contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Error message mentions minimum value of 1", function () {

request.description: Sends page_size=101 to verify the maximum page size validation. Expected response: HTTP 422 with a validation detail for page_size.

exec: pm.test("Validation error contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Error message mentions maximum value of 100", function () {

request.description: Sends an unsupported sort value to verify enum validation. Expected response: HTTP 422 mentioning the accepted asc or desc values.

exec: pm.test("Validation error contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Error message mentions accepted sort values", function () {

request.description: Sends start_time without timezone information. Expected response: HTTP 422 with a timezone validation detail.

exec: pm.test("Validation error contract is valid", function () {

request.description: Sends start_time greater than end_time. Expected response: HTTP 422 with a time-range validation detail.

exec: pm.test("Validation error contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Error message mentions start_time and end_time", function () {

request.description: Sends min_magnitude greater than max_magnitude. Expected response: HTTP 422 with a magnitude-range validation detail.

exec: pm.test("Validation error contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Error message mentions min_magnitude and max_magnitude", function () {

request.description: Sends an unsupported query parameter. Expected response: HTTP 422 indicating that extra query inputs are forbidden.

exec: pm.test("Validation error contract is valid", function () {
pm.test("Error location contains unknown parameter", function () {
pm.test("Error indicates extra inputs are forbidden", function () {
